rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF OKSOON JAE CORTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE CONSERVATORSHIP of Person and Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/4/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/19/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SUZANNE BETTE WEINBERG 2018 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas For Consumer Records (Wells Fargo, Bank Of America Credit Cards)
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondents Simon and Sebastian Weinberg's Motion to Quash Subpoena for Consumer Records is GRANTED on the basis of insufficient notice under CCP section 1985.3(b). A party seeking consumer-related records must serve the consumer with notice of the subpoena. (CCP section 1985.3(b)(3).) A "consumer" may be "any [] trust which has transacted business with, or has used the services of, the witness or for whom the witness has acted as agent or fiduciary." (CCP section 1985.3(a)(2).) If the subpoenaed party is a witness and the subpoenaed records pertain to a consumer, "the service of the deposition subpoena shall be accompanied either by a copy of the proof of service of the notice to the consumer [per CCP section 1985.3(e)] or by the consumer's written authorization to release personal records described in [CCP section 1985.3(c)(2)]." (CCP sections 1985.3(a)(1), 2020.410(d).) The intent of the service/consent rules is to ensure that the consumer is notified of the subpoena and has an opportunity to challenge it. "As a general rule, the trustee is the real party in interest with standing to sue or defend on the trust's behalf. [Citations.]" (Estate of Bowles (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 684, 691.) Here, Terry Weinberg served a subpoena on the banks, seeking records regarding accounts titled to the Murray Weinberg and S. Bette Weinberg 1984 Trust ("1984 Trust"). The 1984 Trust is the "consumer" in this instance. Terry served the subpoena at a time when the 1984 Trust was without a trustee. It follows that no trustee was served with notice of the subpoena and no trustee consented to release of the information. On the circumstances of this case, service on the beneficiaries does not satisfy the statutory notice requirement because the beneficiaries are not in a position to advocate for or defend the trust itself. Service of notice of the subpoena is thus incomplete and does not comply with the statutory requirements. The Court need not - and does not - make any findings as to the scope or breadth of the subpoenaed records. The successor trustee, once they are properly served notice of the subpoena, may decide whether or not to lodge further objections to the subpoena on behalf of the 1984 Trust. Respondents shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on July 6, 2021. Remote appearance is available, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CRAIG RICHARD PAYNE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: continued to 8/4/21. as noted in the original notes, the proof of publication had not yet been filed, so it had not been reviewed. on review, however, the first publication date was prior to the amendment being filed (the amendment got a filing date of 5/11/21 based on the date it was uploaded, but it was not actually filed until 5/12/21). Notice (of which publication is one form) is not proper prior to the filing of the document that requires notice (LRSF 14.14.A.). New publication is required. filing deadline for the new POP is noon on 7/26/21. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to review under LRSF 14.10.
